<recommended_plugins>
Here is a list of plugins that are available but not installed. If the user's query would benefit from one of these plugins, use the `request_plugin_install` tool to suggest that they install it. Pass the parenthesized ID as `plugin_id`. For example, suggest the Google Drive plugin if the query could possibly be better answered with access to Google Drive.

- Atlassian Rovo (atlassian-rovo@openai-curated-remote)
- Box (box@openai-curated-remote)
- Gmail (gmail@openai-curated-remote)
- Google Calendar (google-calendar@openai-curated-remote)
- Outlook Calendar (outlook-calendar@openai-curated-remote)
- Outlook Email (outlook-email@openai-curated-remote)
- SharePoint (sharepoint@openai-curated-remote)
- Teams (teams@openai-curated-remote)
</recommended_plugins>
i want you to go through the codebase with 10 different code-reaper subagents and tell me what we should clean up in this codebase to make it more maintainable.

Ten independent code-reaper audits are complete: architecture, duplication, dead code, contracts, errors/concurrency, tests, frontend, persistence, CLI/Git, and tooling. The worktree remains clean; no code was changed.

There are no P0 findings. The largest maintainability problem is persistence complexity, followed by domain rules leaking into adapters.

## Highest-priority cleanup

1. Collapse persistence to one asynchronous Postgres implementation.

   - [`DbRuntime`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/runtime.rs:3) creates a private Tokio runtime, blocks callers through a synchronous channel, and deliberately leaks the runtime.
   - Approximately 65 branches separately implement memory and Postgres behavior; they already disagree on request ordering.
   - One global `"catalog"` lock serializes unrelated writes.
   - [`MetadataStore::read`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:136) loads the entire database for narrow operations.

   Recommendation: delete `memory-metadata`, run local development/tests on disposable Postgres, make store methods async, replace whole-catalog reads with focused queries, and gradually replace the global lock with aggregate/row-level locking.

2. Restore an actual delivery-independent domain.

   - [`scope-core/error.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/error.rs:1) depends on Axum and HTTP status codes.
   - Request visibility, permissions, and mergeability live in [`api/http/requests.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/requests.rs:537), while related authorization is duplicated in persistence.
   - Repository path authorization is copied between [`domain/store.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:416) and [`api/state.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/state.rs:456).
   - Settlement policy is duplicated in Rust and [`request-labels.ts`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/features/requests/request-labels.ts:220).

   Recommendation: introduce neutral domain errors, domain-owned request access decisions, push authorization, and settlement previews. HTTP, CLI, persistence, and web should only translate those results.

3. Delete two unowned feature slices.

   - `PendingImport` has no production constructor assigning `Some`; only tests manufacture it. Nevertheless it spans domain models, a database column, response fields, CLI branching, and UI.
   - The backend `RepoSettings` fields/table have no production readers or update route. This is separate from the current collaboration/settings UI.

   Recommendation: destructively remove both vertical slices, their persistence plumbing, generated contract fields, artificial tests, and the obsolete-route tombstone test.

4. Fix side-effect and resource choreography.

   - Filesystem/S3 deletion occurs inside a database transaction while holding the global write lock in [`cleanup_queue.rs`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/cleanup_queue.rs:60).
   - [`handle_git_receive_pack`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/mod.rs:470) manually deletes its staging directory roughly twenty times; two `?` paths bypass cleanup.
   - Cleanup retry fields exist but are not used for due-time filtering or backoff.

   Recommendation: claim work in a short transaction, perform external I/O outside it, then reconcile using generation tokens. Give Git staging directories an RAII cleanup guard. Either finish retry semantics or delete the misleading fields.

5. Establish one transport-contract source.

   API DTOs and routes are copied into the CLI, with only auth covered by drift tests. Some shapes have already diverged. Resolve inputs also accept `Accepted` even though the domain rejects direct acceptance.

   Recommendation: create a small API transport-contract crate shared by API and CLI, continue generating TypeScript from it, introduce a narrower resolution disposition type, and add a typed Git OID value.

## Secondary cleanup

6. Simplify frontend state ownership.

   - The repository layout and settings child both start [`useRepoLiveRefresh`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/features/repo-detail/repo-live-refresh.ts:16), creating duplicate SSE connections and invalidations.
   - The 833-line history module has both route-loader and client-effect fetch ownership.
   - Settings combines loader data with a manually reconciled local copy.
   - History and request detail should be split by behavior, not merely file size.

7. Make persistence mutation-specific.

   [`save_repository_row`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repository_rows.rs:49) rewrites the entire repository aggregate and schedules projection work even for invite/settings changes. Persist deltas owned by each command. Also replace saturating database conversions with checked conversions and choose one schema source instead of maintaining entities plus a large custom schema contract.

8. Thin the CLI.

   Delete the unused `push_authenticated_remote` workflow and dead changed-path helpers. Split the 874-line binary into plain `init`, `push`, and `login` command modules. Consolidate duplicated remote parsing, Git auth plans, process execution, and the double request/preflight dispatch.

9. Reorganize tests around ownership.

   - Move domain tests from `api/tests` into `scope-core/tests`.
   - Move the 11,000-line API test tree out of `api/src`.
   - Delete the 491-line handwritten Rust lexer once crate structure enforces boundaries.
   - Replace copied/manual temp-directory code with `tempfile::TempDir`.
   - Add a small Playwright smoke suite for critical UI workflows.

10. Take the tooling quick wins.

   - Generate the CLI build matrix and expected artifacts from [`targets.json`](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/distribution/targets.json:2).
   - Run the web production build on PRs.
   - Make CI invoke the canonical `pnpm check`.
   - Remove duplicated Railway upload trees.
   - Remove verified-unused direct dependencies and redundant toolchain pins.
   - Delete the stale React Doctor suppression.

## Recommended execution order

1. Duplicate SSE, RAII staging cleanup, CI quick wins.
2. Delete pending-import and backend repo-settings slices.
3. Postgres-only async persistence and targeted queries.
4. Domain errors, authorization, settlement, and shared contracts.
5. Frontend/CLI/test modularization.

Keep the explicit `RepoMutation`/`RepoEffects` pattern, generated TypeScript drift check, durable cleanup/outbox records, safe Git subprocess runner, pure file-tree model, and justified cross-platform CLI build matrix. Those are sound foundations rather than slop.

This was a source-backed read-only audit; I validated the leading findings with repository-wide searches and direct inspection, but did not run the full test suite because nothing changed.

yeah this sounds good. go ahead and make an html plan for this along with an expected line number decrease as a part of this pass. will be rewarded for lowering the line count significantly while ensuring behavior is kept.

Which LOC metric should the plan use?

1. Hand-owned production + test code, excluding generated files/docs — recommended.
2. Production code only.
3. All tracked code, including generated files and tests.

Current baseline: 66,594 hand-owned source and test lines.

Three planning approaches:

1. Deletion-first campaign — recommended  
   Lock behavior with targeted tests, remove dead vertical slices, collapse persistence, consolidate domain/contracts, then simplify UI/CLI/tests. Set a hard expected reduction of 8,000–10,000 lines, with a minimum acceptable result of 7,000 lines.

2. Subsystem-by-subsystem  
   Separate persistence, domain, frontend, CLI, and tests into independent projects. Lower risk, but duplicated machinery survives longer and expected reduction falls to roughly 5,000–7,000 lines.

3. LOC-maximizing sweep  
   Target 10,000+ lines immediately through broad test and abstraction removal. Fastest reduction, but creates unacceptable risk of deleting regression protection or hiding complexity.

I recommend option 1, targeting a final baseline of 56,500–58,500 lines while requiring unchanged behavior and passing checks after every phase. Should I use that structure and target for the HTML plan?
yes go ahead and plan out number 1.

Created the deletion-first HTML plan:

[Codebase Maintainability and Line Reduction Plan](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-09-codebase-maintainability-reduction-plan.html)

It includes:

- 66,594-line baseline and reproducible measurement command
- Expected 8,000–10,000 net reduction
- 7,000-line minimum success threshold
- Per-phase delete/add/net budgets
- Behavior-preservation gates
- Seven-phase implementation sequence
- Verification, risk, rollback, and closeout criteria

Static validation and self-review passed. Browser preview tooling was unavailable, so rendered appearance still needs review. I left the existing unrelated `AGENTS.md` modification untouched and did not commit anything.

Please review the HTML plan and let me know what you want adjusted before implementation.
please give me the link
[Open the Codebase Maintainability and Line Reduction Plan](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-09-codebase-maintainability-reduction-plan.html)

content.text:
Audit complete; no files edited.

Unmet test/tooling/CI items:

- CLI matrix and expected artifacts remain hard-coded in the workflow instead of generated from `targets.json`.
- API workflow tests remain inside `api/src/tests`: 28 files, 10,652 LOC, with no narrow public integration harness.
- Three durable domain test files remain under `api/tests` and import `api::domain` rather than `scope_core`.
- Test fixtures still use 86 manual `remove_dir_all` calls and 21 direct temp-directory constructions rather than shared RAII fixtures.
- No Playwright smoke suite exists; Playwright is only used by the standalone UI-audit script.
- Rust workspace check consolidation is partial: API CI checks all workspace packages, while worker CI repeats formatting and core/worker tests and linting.
- Contract drift checking still excludes the handwritten SSE payload and relies on separate CLI DTO comparison rather than one shared transport owner.

Already satisfied:

- Web CI uses canonical `pnpm check` and runs production builds on pull requests.
- Duplicate Railway upload roots were removed.
- Redundant package-level Rust toolchain files were removed.
- The handwritten Rust source lexer was removed and replaced with a core boundary test.

I’ll add a small Node test suite driven by the existing Playwright package. It will target the seeded public repository through `SCOPE_WEB_BASE_URL`/`PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL`, keeping `pnpm test` independent of a running stack.

input.input: const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"rg -n \"public-demo|dev/public|seed|SCOPE_WEB|BASE_URL|3000|5173\" dev web README.md .env.example -g '!web/node_modules/**' -g '!web/.output/**' -g '!web/.test-output/**' | head -n 260; sed -n '1,220p' dev/README.md; sed -n '1,220p' web/src/routes/repos.\\$owner.\\$repo.index.tsx; sed -n '1,180p' web/src/routes/repos.\\$owner.\\$repo.history.tsx; sed -n '1,150p' web/src/routes/repos.\\$owner.\\$repo.requests.index.tsx","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r);

const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"rg -n \"<h1|<h2|PageHeader|title=|History|Requests|Repository|README|Code\" web/src/features/repo-detail/repo-detail-page.tsx web/src/features/history/history-page.tsx web/src/features/requests/requests-page.tsx web/src/routes/repos.\\$owner.\\$repo.tsx | head -n 260; sed -n '1,260p' web/src/features/repo-detail/repo-detail-page.tsx; sed -n '1,260p' web/src/features/requests/requests-page.tsx","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r);

const r = await tools.shell_command({command:"sed -n '1,180p' web/src/components/page-header.tsx; sed -n '1,180p' web/src/components/repo-shell.tsx; sed -n '1,180p' web/src/features/repo-detail/repository-code-view.tsx; sed -n '1,150p' dev/api/seed.rs","workdir":"/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs","timeout_ms":10000}); text(r);

const patch = "*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/package.json
@@
     "start": "node .output/server/index.mjs",
     "test": "node -e \"const fs=require('node:fs'); fs.rmSync('.test-output',{recursive:true,force:true}); fs.mkdirSync('.test-output',{recursive:true}); fs.writeFileSync('.test-output/package.json','{\\\"type\\\":\\\"commonjs\\\"}')\" && tsc -p tsconfig.test.json && node --test \".test-output/**/*.test.js\"",
+    "test:smoke": "node --test \"smoke/**/*.spec.mjs\"",
     "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
*** Add File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/smoke/public-repository.spec.mjs
+import assert from 'node:assert/strict'
+import { after, before, test } from 'node:test'
+import { chromium } from 'playwright'
+
+const baseUrl = (
+  process.env.SCOPE_WEB_BASE_URL ??
+  process.env.PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL ??
+  'http://localhost:3000'
+).replace(/\/$/, '')
+const repoId = process.env.SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO ?? process.env.UI_AUDIT_REPO ?? 'dev/public-demo'
+const [owner, repo, extra] = repoId.split('/')
+
+if (!owner || !repo || extra) {
+  throw new Error('SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO must be an owner/repository pair')
+}
+
+const repoPath = `/repos/${encodeURIComponent(owner)}/${encodeURIComponent(repo)}`
+let browser
+
+before(async () => {
+  browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true })
+})
+
+after(async () => {
+  await browser?.close()
+})
+
+test('public repository exposes only its projected source', async () => {
+  await withPage(repoPath, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: repoId }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'Code')
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 2, name: 'Source' }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('README.md', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('app.ts', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+    assert.equal(await page.getByText('internal', { exact: true }).count(), 0)
+    assert.equal(await page.getByText('plan.md', { exact: true }).count(), 0)
+  })
+})
+
+test('public repository history renders its seeded commit', async () => {
+  await withPage(`${repoPath}/history`, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: 'History' }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'History')
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 2, name: 'Commits' }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('Seed public demo', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+  })
+})
+
+test('public repository requests route is anonymously readable', async () => {
+  await withPage(`${repoPath}/requests`, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: 'Requests' }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'Requests')
+    await page.getByText('No requests yet', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+  })
+})
+
+async function withPage(path, assertion) {
+  const page = await browser.newPage()
+  const pageErrors = []
+  page.on('pageerror', (error) => pageErrors.push(error.message))
+
+  try {
+    const response = await page.goto(new URL(path, `${baseUrl}/`).toString(), {
+      timeout: 30_000,
+      waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',
+    })
+    assert(response, `navigation to ${path} did not produce a response`)
+    assert(response.status() < 400, `navigation to ${path} returned ${response.status()}`)
+    await assertion(page)
+    assert.deepEqual(pageErrors, [])
+  } finally {
+    await page.close()
+  }
+}
+
+async function assertCurrentRepoSection(page, section) {
+  const link = page
+    .getByRole('navigation', { name: 'Repository' })
+    .getByRole('link', { name: section, exact: true })
+  await link.waitFor()
+  assert.equal(await link.getAttribute('aria-current'), 'page')
+}
*** Update File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/dev/README.md
@@
 Run `./dev/scope-dev bench` while the stack is up to collect the Phase 0 local
 data-architecture baseline. The benchmark harness lives under `bench/`, uses
 the seeded local repos, reuses the local Scope CLI session when available, and
 writes ignored reports under `.tmp/bench/phase0/`.
+
+With the stack running, exercise the highest-value anonymous web routes with:
+
+```bash
+(cd web && pnpm test:smoke)
+```
+
+The smoke suite defaults to `http://localhost:3000` and `dev/public-demo`.
+Set `SCOPE_WEB_BASE_URL` to a Tailscale URL or `SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO` to another
+seeded `owner/repository` pair when testing a remote development stack.
*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

     "start": "node .output/server/index.mjs",
     "test": "node -e \"const fs=require('node:fs'); fs.rmSync('.test-output',{recursive:true,force:true}); fs.mkdirSync('.test-output',{recursive:true}); fs.writeFileSync('.test-output/package.json','{\\\"type\\\":\\\"commonjs\\\"}')\" && tsc -p tsconfig.test.json && node --test \".test-output/**/*.test.js\"",
+    "test:smoke": "node --test \"smoke/**/*.spec.mjs\"",

*** Add File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/smoke/public-repository.spec.mjs
+import assert from 'node:assert/strict'
+import { after, before, test } from 'node:test'
+import { chromium } from 'playwright'
+
+const baseUrl = (
+  process.env.SCOPE_WEB_BASE_URL ??
+  process.env.PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL ??
+  'http://localhost:3000'
+).replace(/\/$/, '')
+const repoId = process.env.SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO ?? process.env.UI_AUDIT_REPO ?? 'dev/public-demo'
+const [owner, repo, extra] = repoId.split('/')
+
+if (!owner || !repo || extra) {
+  throw new Error('SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO must be an owner/repository pair')
+}
+
+const repoPath = `/repos/${encodeURIComponent(owner)}/${encodeURIComponent(repo)}`
+let browser
+
+before(async () => {
+  browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true })
+})
+
+after(async () => {
+  await browser?.close()
+})
+
+test('public repository exposes only its projected source', async () => {
+  await withPage(repoPath, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: repoId }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'Code')
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 2, name: 'Source' }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('README.md', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('app.ts', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+    assert.equal(await page.getByText('internal', { exact: true }).count(), 0)
+    assert.equal(await page.getByText('plan.md', { exact: true }).count(), 0)
+  })
+})
+
+test('public repository history renders its seeded commit', async () => {
+  await withPage(`${repoPath}/history`, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: 'History' }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'History')
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 2, name: 'Commits' }).waitFor()
+    await page.getByText('Seed public demo', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+  })
+})
+
+test('public repository requests route is anonymously readable', async () => {
+  await withPage(`${repoPath}/requests`, async (page) => {
+    await page.getByRole('heading', { level: 1, name: 'Requests' }).waitFor()
+    await assertCurrentRepoSection(page, 'Requests')
+    await page.getByText('No requests yet', { exact: true }).waitFor()
+  })
+})
+
+async function withPage(path, assertion) {
+  const page = await browser.newPage()
+  const pageErrors = []
+  page.on('pageerror', (error) => pageErrors.push(error.message))
+
+  try {
+    const response = await page.goto(new URL(path, `${baseUrl}/`).toString(), {
+      timeout: 30_000,
+      waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',
+    })
+    assert(response, `navigation to ${path} did not produce a response`)
+    assert(response.status() < 400, `navigation to ${path} returned ${response.status()}`)
+    await assertion(page)
+    assert.deepEqual(pageErrors, [])
+  } finally {
+    await page.close()
+  }
+}
+
+async function assertCurrentRepoSection(page, section) {
+  const link = page
+    .getByRole('navigation', { name: 'Repository' })
+    .getByRole('link', { name: section, exact: true })
+  await link.waitFor()
+  assert.equal(await link.getAttribute('aria-current'), 'page')
+}
*** Update File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/dev/README.md
@@
 writes ignored reports to `.tmp/bench/phase0/`.
+
+With the stack running, exercise the highest-value anonymous web routes with:
+

+(cd web && pnpm test:smoke)

+The smoke suite defaults to `http://localhost:3000` and `dev/public-demo`.
+Set `SCOPE_WEB_BASE_URL` to a Tailscale URL or `SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO` to another
+seeded `owner/repository` pair when testing a remote development stack.

content.text: The browser runner itself works, but the processes occupying ports 3000/8080 are outside the dev supervisor and every seeded route returns HTTP 500. I’m treating that as a stack-state failure, not weakening the assertions; I’ll verify the suite against a clean supervised stack if the current processes can be identified safely.

content.text: Root cause confirmed: both occupied ports belong to a different worktree, and its API returns 404 for the expected seeded repository; the smoke assertions correctly surface that as SSR 500s. I’m leaving those processes untouched and validating that the new opt-in suite does not disturb the canonical web checks.

content.text: A dry-list check exposed one test-harness flaw: a file-level browser hook can hang when Node filters out every test, because setup runs without a matching teardown path. I’m switching to per-test browser ownership so filtered runs and individual failures always clean up their process.

content.text: The focused run found two assertion assumptions, not application failures: the source tree intentionally collapses `src`, so `app.ts` is not initially visible, and public projection intentionally sanitizes the logical commit message to “Projected public update.” I’m updating the smoke checks to assert stable public behavior—file count, visible README/private-path exclusion, projected commit ID and sanitized label.

content.text:
Implemented and verified the minimal Playwright smoke layer.

Changes:

- Added `pnpm test:smoke`.
- Added three anonymous browser checks covering:
  - Public projected source and private-path exclusion.
  - Public projected commit history.
  - Anonymous requests route.
- Added `SCOPE_WEB_BASE_URL`/`PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL` and `SCOPE_SMOKE_REPO` overrides.
- Documented localhost and Tailscale usage.
- Kept smoke tests separate from canonical unit tests.

Verification: all 3 smoke tests passed against the available seeded development stack.

I’m extending the existing Rust-to-TypeScript exporter rather than adding a second generator. The web parser will keep its defensive runtime checks; only the compile-time event shape will move to the generated contract.

content.text: The CI duplication has a safe consolidation point: both jobs use the same Postgres service, toolchain, cache, format/test/clippy sequence, and only differ by whether API and contract checks are included. I’m replacing those copied jobs with one local reusable workflow controlled by a boolean, leaving each deployment workflow and its trigger semantics intact.

const patch = "*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/.github/workflows/rust-workspace-checks.yml
+name: Rust Workspace Checks
+
+on:
+  workflow_call:
+    inputs:
+      cache-scope:
+        required: true
+        type: string
+      include-api:
+        default: false
+        required: false
+        type: boolean
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true
+  SCOPE_TEST_DATABASE_URL: REDACTED  checks:
+    name: Check scope-${{ inputs.cache-scope }}
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    services:
+      postgres:
+        image: postgres:16
+        env:
+          POSTGRES_DB: scope_test
+          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: scope
+          POSTGRES_USER: scope
+        ports:
+          - 5432:5432
+        options: >-
+          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U scope -d scope_test"
+          --health-interval 5s
+          --health-timeout 5s
+          --health-retries 10
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v7
+
+      - name: Install Rust
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
+        with:
+          toolchain: 1.94.0
+          components: rustfmt, clippy
+
+      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
+        uses: actions/cache@v6
+        with:
+          path: |
+            ~/.cargo/git
+            ~/.cargo/registry
+            target
+          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ inputs.cache-scope }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
+          restore-keys: |
+            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ inputs.cache-scope }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}
+            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ inputs.cache-scope }}-cargo-
+
+      - name: Check formatting
+        run: cargo fmt --all --check
+
+      - name: Test API workspace
+        if: inputs.include-api
+        run: cargo test -p scope-core -p api -p worker --locked
+
+      - name: Test worker workspace
+        if: ${{ !inputs.include-api }}
+        run: cargo test -p scope-core -p worker --locked
+
+      - name: Lint API workspace
+        if: inputs.include-api
+        run: cargo clippy -p scope-core -p api -p worker --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings
+
+      - name: Lint worker workspace
+        if: ${{ !inputs.include-api }}
+        run: cargo clippy -p scope-core -p worker --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings
+
+      - name: Install Node
+        if: inputs.include-api
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
+        with:
+          node-version: 24
+
+      - name: Check API TypeScript contract
+        if: inputs.include-api
+        working-directory: web
+        run: node scripts/check-api-contract.mjs
*** Update File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/.github/workflows/scope-api-ci.yml

content.text:
Completed the contract/tooling slice.

- `RepoChangeEvent` is now generated from Rust into TypeScript.
- Web live refresh imports the generated type and retains runtime payload validation.
- Existing stale-contract exporter automatically covers SSE.
- API and worker CI now share one reusable Postgres-backed Rust workspace check workflow.
- Web checks and all 28 tests pass.
- Exporter test and YAML validation pass.
- `git diff --check` passes.

The final workspace format check only reported an unrelated concurrent formatting change in `api/src/tests/git_receive_config.rs`; I left it untouched.
